summary: Closed, unmerged HSD/baselib decomp PR focused mainly on src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c, with header prototype/type updates, psdisp formatting-only style churn, and small formatting cleanups in gm and gr files. Bot evidence reports one new match, fn_803AC3F8, plus improvements to fn_803AE7F8, fn_803ADF90, and fn_803B0E9C, with a small regression in fn_803B21E8. The author later stated the PR was superseded by PR #2512.

key_files.role: Primary decomp target; adds local structs/macros, includes hsd_3B2B.h, rewrites fn_803AC3F8 to a matching loop form, and adds or improves large CARD/HSD functions including fn_803ADF90 and fn_803AE7F8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3AA7.c include hsd_3B2B.h, HsdCmdEntry, CardStateExt, CMD_QUEUE, fn_803AC3F8 rewrite, fn_803ADF90/fn_803AE7F8 additions
text_corpus bot report: fn_803AC3F8 82.55% to 100.00%; fn_803AE7F8, fn_803ADF90, fn_803B0E9C improved; fn_803B21E8 regressed

key_files.role: Header surface updated with more concrete struct fields and prototypes for recovered hsd_3AA7 functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803AAA48 changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 hsd_803AAA48(void); fn_803AD16C and several fn_803ADF90-family prototypes updated to typed arguments

key_files.role: Formatting/include-order cleanup around psDispParticles; no match claim specific to this file in loaded bot report.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: forward.h include moved after cobj/fog; long GX, cache, and color expressions split across lines

key_files.role: Style-only compound literal spacing in fn_8018DF68.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: GXColor compound literals changed from (GXColor){...} to (GXColor) {...}

key_files.role: Style-only function pointer cast spacing in gmClassic_801B3500.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: casts changed from u8(*)(...) to u8 (*)(...) and f32(*)(...) to f32 (*)(...)

key_files.role: Style-only function pointer cast spacing in grIceMt_801FA364.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: cb_ cast spacing changed to s32 (*)(HSD_GObj*, s32*)

what_changed: Replaced the placeholder for fn_803AC3F8 with a cleaner loop-based implementation that the bot report says became a 100.00% match.
Added temporary/local HSD CARD-related structures such as HsdCmdEntry, Hsd803B2550Ctx, and CardStateExt, plus CMD_QUEUE, to express offsets used by hsd_3AA7.c.
Added large hsd_3AA7.c implementations or partial implementations for CARD block/file operations, with visible additions for fn_803ADF90 and fn_803AE7F8 and bot-reported improvement for fn_803B0E9C.
Updated hsd_3AA7.h prototypes from unknown or generic s32 signatures toward typed CardState or hsd_803AC3E0_arg0_t pointer signatures.
Applied formatting changes in psdisp.c and small cast/compound-literal spacing changes in gm_18A5.c, gmclassic.c, and gricemt.c.
The PR was closed unmerged and later marked by the author as superseded by PR #2512.
smart_moves: The fn_803AC3F8 rewrite removed generated-looking unrolled zero-fill code and used bounded loops, aligning with the bot-reported new 100% function match.
The header changes moved several prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or fully untyped s32 arguments, making call surfaces more reviewable when the body evidence supported the argument shape.
The hsd_3AA7.c changes used named local structures and arrays for CARD state/block maps rather than leaving the whole implementation as raw placeholder declarations.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small fixed-size record writers, try a natural loop plus a cleanup loop before preserving generated unrolled stores; in this slice, replacing the unrolled zero-fill tail in fn_803AC3F8 with a for-loop form corresponds to the bot-reported 100% match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_803AC3F8 removes remaining/unroll block and uses for (; i < 3; i++) zeroing

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves argument roles, update the owning header instead of leaving generic prototypes; this PR updates hsd_3AA7.h signatures for hsd_803AAA48, fn_803AD16C, fn_803ADF90-family functions, and fn_803B21E8.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3AA7.h prototype changes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and s32-only signatures to typed signatures

naming_conventions.convention: Prefer role-bearing local names such as file_idx, blocks_before, file_blocks, block_map, callback_seq, data_block, and remaining when the decompiled control flow exposes the role.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_803AC3F8 renames idx parameter to file_idx while retaining inner idx for derived file index
raw_diff: fn_803ADF90 locals include block_map, blocks_before, callback_seq, file_blocks, data_block, remaining

naming_conventions.convention: This slice follows a spacing style for function pointer casts with a space between return type and (*), e.g. u8 (*)(...) and s32 (*)(...).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmclassic.c function pointer cast spacing

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Natural loop reconstruction for repeated stores can improve match quality; fn_803AC3F8 is the clearest supported example in this PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_803AC3F8 unrolled zero stores replaced by for-loop zeroing

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Document non-final partial matches with narrow todo notes that identify the codegen mismatch, such as volatile load scheduling, rlwimi form, or stwx versus stw+disp.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3AA7.c todo comments mention 91.67% volatile load scheduling, 76.6% combined rlwimi, and 93.46% stwx vs stw+disp/register swap

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use temporary local structs to express known offset regions during large baselib/CARD recovery, but treat them as provisional unless later evidence promotes them into stable headers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3AA7.c adds HsdCmdEntry, Hsd803B2550Ctx, CardStateExt in the C file

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot reported matched code 67.19% (+0.01%, +352 bytes), one new match fn_803AC3F8, improvements to fn_803AE7F8, fn_803ADF90, and fn_803B0E9C, and a regression in fn_803B21E8 from 94.85% to 92.73%.
Author comment says the PR was superseded by https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/2512.
No loaded human review comments or reviews are present for this slice.

follow_up_queries: hsd_3AA7 volatile load scheduling critical section

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, changed files, counts
raw_diff excerpt and loaded raw_diff: concrete source changes
text_corpus: bot report and superseded author comment
notes: The raw diff is loaded but truncated in the main context; targeted local reads supplied hsd_3AA7.h and psdisp.c details.
No human review comments were loaded, so reviewer intent or acceptance cannot be inferred.
The PR is closed with merged_at null; do not treat these changes as merged or accepted.
The human_pr_text excerpt lists Author: jellejurre, while metadata/raw PR user is jurrejelle; the record preserves metadata author and notes jellejurre only as a searchable term/comment author.
The bot report provides match/regression evidence, but no local build command, objdiff invocation, or final validation ledger is present in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: fn_803AC3F8 matched by replacing generated unrolled zero-fill with natural loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2478, hsd_3AA7.c rewrote fn_803AC3F8 from a generated-looking unrolled zero-fill sequence into bounded for-loops. The bot report says fn_803AC3F8 improved from 82.55% to 100.00%, making this a concrete example of the natural-loops standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes should track recovered hsd_3AA7 body evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2478 updated hsd_3AA7.h from unknown or generic prototypes to typed signatures for several recovered functions, matching the truthful-headers standard when body evidence supports argument roles.

source_update_candidates.title: Record PR #2478 as superseded by PR #2512 in PR-source metadata if a supersession index exists
source_update_candidates.text: The author comment in PR #2478 states: Superseded by https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/2512. This should be represented only as PR relationship metadata, not as evidence that #2512 accepted all changes.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for curator navigation and avoiding promotion of this closed, unmerged slice as accepted final work.

rejection_notes: Do not promote CardStateExt, HsdCmdEntry, or CMD_QUEUE as canonical project types from this PR alone; they are C-file-local in a closed unmerged PR and may have changed in the superseding PR.
Do not claim psdisp.c made decomp progress; loaded evidence shows formatting/include-order churn and no bot-reported psdisp match.
Do not treat the reported fn_803B21E8 state as an improvement; the bot report lists it as a regression.
Do not infer reviewer approval or merge acceptance; the slice has no reviews and merged_at is null.
